description: The Azerbaijani mafia is a general term for organized criminal gangs consisting of ethnic Azerbaijanis. Based primarily in Moscow and other major Russian cities, they are active in former Soviet states, Turkey, the Netherlands, Germany, and other European countries. They emerged in the early 1980s and rose to power in the 1990s by taking over heroin trafficking from weakened Chechen groups. Their activities include drug trafficking, arms trafficking, fraud, money laundering, and contract killing.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2018, a scandal occurred in the south of France : the incident took place in Colomiers , near Toulouse . A car with Rahim Namazov, an Azerbaijani national, and his wife was shot at. The woman died in the attack. This event revealed the very threat of the Azerbaijani criminal groups in the heart of Europe.
country_links.context: The Azerbaijani mafia carried out a shooting attack in Colomiers, France.

country_links.evidence_quote: Ponichala , an Azerbaijani village within Tbilisi , is one of the drug trafficking hubs in Georgia.
country_links.context: The Azerbaijani mafia is active in Georgia, involved in drug trafficking and extortion.

country_links.evidence_quote: Outside of Russia, they are active in most former Soviet states, Turkey , the Netherlands , Germany and other European countries.
country_links.context: The Azerbaijani mafia is active in Germany.
